prompt: A linear function $f$ satisfies $f(-3) = 11$ and $f(5) = -5$. Which equation correctly defines $f(x)$?
The equation $4(2x - 3) = 3(x + 1) + 5x$ is solved below. Identify the step that contains the first error.
choices: Step 1 — the distributive property was applied incorrectly
Step 2 — like terms on the right were combined incorrectly
Step 3 — the subtraction of $8x$ was applied incorrectly
There is no error; the conclusion is correct

prompt: The steps below solve $3(x - 2) + 4 = 2x + 7$, but they are out of order. Drag them into the correct sequence from first to last.
items: Combine like terms on the left: $3x - 2 = 2x + 7$

prompt: A function $g$ is defined by $g(x) = |2x - 6| - 4$. For which value(s) of $x$ does $g(x) = 0$?
A line has slope $-\dfrac{3}{4}$ and passes through the point $(4, 1)$. Complete the slope-intercept equation of the line.
The sum of three consecutive even integers is $78$. What is the largest of the three integers?
Line $\ell$ passes through $(-1, 4)$ and is perpendicular to the line $3x - y = 7$. What is the $y$-intercept of line $\ell$?
Select ALL expressions equivalent to $(3x^2 y^{-1})^2 \cdot (x^{-3} y^2)^3$.
A rideshare company charges a flat booking fee plus a per-mile rate. A 4-mile trip costs $\$8.60$ and an 11-mile trip costs $\$15.25$. What is the per-mile rate?
Two cars depart from the same point in opposite directions. Car A travels at $55$ mph and Car B travels at $70$ mph. After how many minutes will the cars be exactly $62.5$ miles apart?
A manufacturer models profit $P(x) = 12x - (180 + 4x)$. The break-even occurs when $P(x) = 0$. How many additional units beyond break-even must be produced to earn at least $\$200$ profit?
The system below models a real-world scenario. Which statement correctly interprets the solution?
info_box: where $x$ = adult tickets and $y$ = child tickets sold.
choices: $40$ adult and $80$ child tickets were sold
$80$ adult and $40$ child tickets were sold
$60$ adult and $60$ child tickets were sold
$70$ adult and $50$ child tickets were sold

prompt: Match each system of equations to the correct description of its solution type.
A tank drains at a constant rate. At $t = 0$ it holds $480$ gallons; at $t = 12$ minutes it holds $336$ gallons. At what time (minutes) will it reach exactly $60$ gallons?
A contractor charges $\$45$/hr for labor plus $\$320$ in materials. A client's budget is at most $\$1{,}010$. Which inequality models the maximum labor hours $h$?
A savings account balance after $w$ weeks is modeled by $B = 1{,}240 - 65w$. Which statement BEST interprets the slope?
choices: The account begins with $\$65$ and increases by $\$1{,}240$ each week
The account loses $\$65$ in value every week
After $1{,}240$ weeks the account will be empty
The account decreases by $\$1{,}240$ for every $\$65$ withdrawn

prompt: The system $6x - 9y = 15$ and $2x - 3y = k$ has infinitely many solutions. What must $k$ equal?
A system of two linear equations is graphed and intersects at exactly one point. Select ALL statements that MUST be true.
choices: The system has a unique solution
Neither line passes through the origin

prompt: A linear function decreases by $9$ when $x$ increases by $4$, and $f(2) = 3$. What is the $y$-intercept?
Solve the system: $\dfrac{2}{3}x + \dfrac{1}{4}y = 5$ and $\dfrac{1}{2}x - \dfrac{3}{4}y = -1$. What is $x + y$?

A meal plan uses Food A ($8\text{g}$ protein, $200$ cal/serving) and Food B ($5\text{g}$ protein, $150$ cal/serving). The plan needs at least $40\text{g}$ protein and under $1{,}050$ calories. Which system models all constraints?
The table shows selected values of a linear function $h$. What is $h(-15)$?
A store: $6$ pens + $4$ notebooks = $\$18.80$; $9$ pens + $2$ notebooks = $\$17.20$. What does $1$ pen + $1$ notebook cost?
Two plans: Plan A costs $\$25 + \$0.10m$; Plan B costs $\$15 + \$0.14m$. Set costs equal to find the break-even point.
template: Break-even at [minutes] minutes. For more minutes, Plan [plan] costs less.

prompt: The solution to $-3 < 2 - x \leq 7$ is graphed on a number line. Which description is correct?
choices: Open circle at $-5$, closed circle at $5$; shaded between
Closed circle at $-5$, open circle at $5$; shaded between
Open circle at $-5$, closed at $5$; shaded outside
Closed circle at $5$, open at $-5$; shaded between

prompt: $f(x) = 3x - 2$ is transformed to $g(x) = 3(x-4)-2$. Which transformation maps $f$ to $g$?
A piecewise function is defined as shown. What is $f(-1) + f(1) + f(4)$?
$g(x) = -2(x+3)^2 + 5$ is built from $f(x) = x^2$. Arrange the four transformations in correct order of application.
A function $f$ has $f(0)=3$, decreasing on $(-\infty,2)$, increasing on $(2,\infty)$, $f(2)=-1$. Select ALL necessarily true statements.
Using the table, what is the average rate of change of $f$ from $x=3$ to $x=7$?
Match each function description to its correct equation.
A linear function passes through the 2nd and 4th quadrants but NOT the 3rd. Which slope/intercept pair is possible?
Starting with $f(x)=x$, three transformations are applied in order (see box). Which equation represents $g$?
The line $y = 2x - 3$ is graphed below. Click the point on the grid that represents the $x$-intercept of this line.
Select ALL representations that define $y$ as a function of $x$.
Revenue $R(t)=8t+30$ and cost $C(t)=5t+60$ (thousands). At what month $t$ does revenue first exceed cost?
choices: $t=6$; costs and revenues equal at month $6$

prompt: For $f(x)=\dfrac{3x-6}{x^2-4}$, how many integer values of $x$ in $[-5, 5]$ make $f(x)$ undefined?
Which statement correctly compares the rates of change of functions $P$, $Q$, and $R$?
Line $\ell$ passes through $(-2,1)$ with slope $\dfrac{3}{2}$. Which labeled point is the $y$-intercept of $\ell$?
info_box: Use the slope and given point to find where the line crosses the $y$-axis.

prompt: Simplify: $(4x^3-9)\div(2x-3)$. Assume $2x-3\neq 0$.
$(2x+5)(3x-4)-(x-3)^2$ written as $ax^2+bx+c$. What is $a+b+c$?
Area of a rectangle is $6x^2+11x-10$; width is $(2x+5)$. What is the length?
Match each quadratic expression to its correct factored form.
Select ALL expressions equal to $\dfrac{(2^3)^4}{4^2 \cdot 2^{-1}}$.
$x^2-14x+49-4y^2$ written as a product of two binomials. What are those factors?
$f(x)=-2x^2+12x-7$ has vertex form $f(x)=a(x-h)^2+k$. Identify the vertex and maximum value.
For which values of $x$ is $x^2-x-12<0$?
The parabola $f(x) = -(x-2)^2 + 4$ is graphed below. Click the vertex of the parabola.
A quadratic has zeros $x=-1$ and $x=5$, leading coefficient $-3$. Which equation defines it?
$x^2+bx+36=0$ has exactly two real solutions. Select ALL integer values of $b$ for which this is true.
Find $3x-2y$ where $(x,y)$ is the solution to the system below.
A pump fills at $r_1$ gal/hr; a drain removes at $r_2$ gal/hr. After $3$ hr: $270$ net gallons added. After $7$ hr: $490$ net gallons. What is the net rate $r_1-r_2$?
To solve $5x+3y=29$ and $2x-6y=-4$ by elimination, arrange the steps in the correct order.
items: Substitute into the first equation to find $y$

prompt: Model $T(d)$: $T(100)=68$; temperature increases $1.5°$F per $10$ ft depth. Select ALL true statements.
choices: Rate of change is $0.15$ °F per foot

prompt: The feasible region for $y \geq \frac{1}{2}x+1$, $y \leq -x+7$, $x \geq 0$ is shaded. Click any point that lies strictly INSIDE the region (not on a boundary).
$f(x)=x^2-8x+c$ has a vertex with $y$-coordinate $-7$. What is $c$?
For what value(s) of $k$ does $(k-3)x=2k-6$ have infinitely many solutions?
Match each real-world scenario to the algebraic model that best represents it.
Feasible region: $y \geq \frac{1}{2}x+1$, $y \leq -x+7$, $x \geq 0$. Select ALL points inside or on the boundary.
January: $3$ Model X + $7$ Model Y = $\$1{,}670$. February: $5$ Model X + $2$ Model Y = $\$1{,}180$. March: $4$ of each. What is March revenue?
A student concludes $\dfrac{x^2-4}{x-2}=x+2$ holds for all real $x$. Which statement is correct?
choices: Correct; every real number satisfies it
Incorrect; $x=2$ is extraneous — it makes the denominator zero
